name: Business Email Compromise (BEC) Report
description: Internal form for employees to report an email that appears to come from a colleague, executive, or vendor making an unusual request — wire transfers, gift cards, payment redirects, changed banking details. High priority because these are active fraud attempts.
title: Report a Suspicious Request from a Colleague or Vendor
intro: Did you get an email that looks like it's from a coworker, executive, or vendor — but the request feels off? Things like a sudden wire transfer, a gift card purchase, a change to banking details, or 'reach me at this new number'? Report it here before you act on it. Even if it turns out to be legitimate, we'd rather check than miss a real fraud attempt. **Do not reply to the email or take any action until we confirm.**
submit_message: Thanks — this is in our high-priority queue. An analyst will reach out shortly. In the meantime, do not reply to the email or take any action it requested.

fields.label: Your email
fields.help: We'll use this to reach you directly.

fields.label: Who does the email claim to be from?
fields.help: The name shown as the sender — for example, your CEO, a coworker, or a vendor.

fields.label: Sender email address
fields.help: The actual 'From' address. Check carefully — is it your company domain, or something slightly different?

fields.label: What is the email asking for?
options: Wire transfer or ACH payment
Buy gift cards
Change vendor or payroll banking details
Send sensitive data (W-2s, employee info, customer list)
Pay an invoice urgently
Reach me on a new phone number / WhatsApp
Buy something on their behalf
Other unusual request

fields.label: Dollar amount or what they're asking for
fields.help: If money is involved, roughly how much? Otherwise, what's the target of the request?
fields.sample_value: $2,000 in Apple gift cards, send codes via email

fields.label: What feels off about the request?
options: Unusually urgent or time-sensitive
Asked to keep it confidential
Sender is unreachable by phone or Teams/Slack
Sender claims to be in a meeting / traveling / on a plane
Writing style doesn't match the person
Reply-To address differs from the From address
Lookalike domain (e.g., 'rn' instead of 'm')
First time they've asked me for something like this
sample_value: Sender claims to be in a meeting / traveling / on a plane

fields.label: Did you take any action yet?
fields.help: Be honest — we need to know so we can help.
options: No, I haven't done anything yet
I replied to the email
I started the wire / payment but haven't sent
I sent money or gift card codes
I shared the data they asked for
I called the number they provided
fields.sample_value: No, I haven't done anything yet

fields.label: Paste the email content
fields.help: Copy the full email body, including any signature.
fields.sample_value:
Hi Jordan,

I'm in back-to-back board meetings today and can't take calls. I need you to pick up $2,000 in Apple gift cards for a client appreciation gift before EOD. Scratch off the backs and email me the codes. Keep this between us until I announce it.

Thanks,
Marcus

fields.label: Attach the original email (.eml or .msg)
fields.help: Optional but helpful. Drag the email from Outlook to your desktop, then upload it.

alert_template.title: Possible BEC from {{reporter_email}} - impersonating {{claimed_sender_name}}
